frameworks.name: 創新提案路徑
frameworks.desc: 理解 Feature I01: Innovate WELL 如何讓專案提出 WELL v2 尚未涵蓋的新介入策略，提案須以 novel way 正向影響 occupants、有 scientific/medical/industry research 佐證，並符合法規與 leading practices。

frameworks.name: 專業參與與教育推廣
frameworks.desc: 掌握 Feature I02: WELL Accredited Professional 與 Feature I03: Experience WELL Certification：專案團隊至少一人取得並維持 WELL AP 認證至 initial certification，並透過 public tours 與教育策略推廣 WELL。

frameworks.name: 既有計畫等同加分
frameworks.desc: 理解 Feature I04: Gateways to Wellness 與 Feature I05: Green Building Rating Systems：以三年內完成的 health and wellness program、或取得 IWBI 認可的 green building rating（如 LEED）換取創新分，I05 最多 5 分且不可疊加。

frameworks.name: 創新計分與驗證
frameworks.desc: Innovation concept 全部為 optimization，專案最多取得 10 分；各 Innovation feature 的 verification method（technical document、letter of assurance 等）與計分上限是常見考點。

questions.stem: How long does the WELL Accredited Professional (WELL AP™) need to maintain their accreditation to be earn the innovation point for Feature I02: WELL Accredited Professional WELL AP?

options.text: For three years after initial certification.
Until the project's initial certification is achieved.
Until materials for initial certification have been submitted.
For six months after initial certification.

questions.stem: How is the innovation point for Feature I02 verified?

options.text: Professional narrative.
Examination by certification authority.
Technical Document
Third party review.

questions.stem: A project can earn an innovation point for spreading awareness of WELL Certification by providing free, public tours of the WELL Certified space. How often are tours offered to the public?

options.text: Annually
Bi Annually
Quarterly
At least six times per year.

questions.source_explanation: Part 1: Offer Educational Tours of Feature I03: Experience WELL Certification.

questions.stem: An office building in Houston is looking for innovation points for Feature I05: Green Building Rating Systems. What is an example of a Green Building Rating System that is approved by IWBI?

options.text: LEED (USGBC).
BREATHE.
Leaf Certification.
Green Houses.

questions.stem: In order to earn the 5 points available for Feature I05: Green Building Rating Systems, what must be done?

options.text: The project must have already applied for LEED Certification
The project must certify in a green building rating system approved by IWBI
The project must apply for atleast 5 different Green Building Certifications
All Of the Above

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for Part 2: Propose Innovation of Feature I01: Innovate WELL?

options.text: Must be consistent with applicable laws and regulations and leading practices in building design and operations.
Must demonstrate existing scientific, medical and/or industry research.
Must submit the outline of time and cost of innovation and its overall impact on the project.
Positively impacts project occupants by supporting health and well-being in a novel way that is not covered in WELL v2.

questions.source_explanation:
As the scientific understanding of health continues to evolve, so too does the ability 
to address the complex issue of promoting health and well-being through building 
design and operations. WELL Innovation features embrace novel approaches to 
promoting the creation of healthier spaces that go above and beyond features in 
WELL v2.

questions.stem: What are some ways a project team can earn an innovation point for Feature I04: Gateway to Wellness?

options.text: Complete an independent health and wellness program within the last three years.
Conduct an annual wellness survey.
Hire a WELL AP to create a wellness program.
Annual health and wellness program.

questions.stem: What is the primary reason that IWBI aligned with Green Building Rating Systems such as LEED and BREEAM?

options.text: To be more attractive to the market.
To help reduce overall costs to the project.
To balance sustainability and human health considerations.
To help reduce greenhouse gasses.

questions.source_explanation: Read overview of Feature I05: Green Building Rating Systems.

questions.stem: A project team wants to receive an innovation point for their WELL Certified Building. What are some of the ways they can do this?

options.text: Offer a public tour that is listed in the WELL Certified spaces directory.
They can advertise WELL Features and Benefits in the community.
Offer a public tour three times per year.
Offer tours on the buildings website up to 6 times per year.

questions.stem: What feature should a project use to submit innovation proposals?

options.text: Feature I03: Experience WELL Certification
Feature I01: Innovate WELL
You do not need to use a feature in order to submit an innovation proposal.
Innovation proposals are submitted via email to IWBI.

questions.stem: How many innovation points can a project receive?

options.text: up to 3 points.
up to 8 points.
up to 10 points.
up to 15 points.

questions.source_explanation: Read the overview for the Innovation Concept.

questions.stem: What is the intent of the Innovation Concept?

options.text: to pave the way for projects to do what they want.
to pave the way for projects to represent different strategies.
to pave the way for projects to create wellness movements in their facilities.
to pave the way for projects to develop unique strategies for creating healthy environments.
